source_title: ExoStart: Efficient learning for dexterous manipulation with sensorized exoskeleton demonstrations

ai_summary.one_liner: ExoStart converts ~10 exoskeleton demonstrations into dexterous robot manipulation policies via dynamics filtering, auto-curriculum RL, and vision-based distillation for zero-shot real-world transfer.
ai_summary.problem: Dexterous manipulation learning requires many demonstrations or expensive RL training; exoskeleton demonstrations are kinematically rich but physically inconsistent with robot dynamics, creating a sim-to-real gap.
ai_summary.solution: Sensorized exoskeleton (kinematically equivalent to robot hand) collects ~10 real demonstrations → MuJoCo dynamics filtering augments them into physically feasible sim trajectories → auto-curriculum RL trains a feature-based teacher policy → vision-based student policy is distilled for zero-shot real deployment.
ai_summary.key_result: >50% real-world success rate on 6 of 7 complex dexterous tasks (teacher 95-99% in sim); 6× faster data collection than teleoperation.
limitations: Per-robot-hand exoskeleton design required; sim-to-real gap from control rate mismatch (200 Hz sim vs 20 Hz real)
Object pose tracking requires AR-tagged replicas; soft-contact model failure causes 2% success on Bulb Install

research_gaps.question: Teacher → Student 증류 과정에서 vision student가 teacher 성능(95~99%)의 절반 수준에 머무는 원인이 sim-to-real 시각 격차인지 행동 복제 capacity 한계인지 분석되지 않아, 향후 개선의 병목이 불명확하다
